summary.intent: Address Codex review feedback on PR #669 in the ars-ui repo by fixing three edge cases in the agnostic combobox core and keeping CI green through the full matrix.
summary.outcome: All four original Codex threads were resolved, three new Codex findings were patched with regression tests added first, the combobox spec was updated to match the new behavior, and the fixes were committed and pushed with all CI gates passing.

repo: The combobox component lives in crates/ars-components/src/selection/combobox/mod.rs and has a companion spec at spec/components/selection/combobox.md that must be kept in sync with behavior changes.
cargo xci --profile fast runs 10 gates (fmt, check, clippy, unit tests, etc.) and is the standard pre-push verification pipeline.
cargo xtask spec validate is used to verify the spec document is consistent with the implementation.
Codex review threads are tracked via GitHub GraphQL API and polled separately from CI checks; the reviewer signals acknowledgment with 👀 reactions.
The project uses a mutual-exclusion guard as one of the CI checks alongside snapshot change checks, workspace checks, clippy, unit tests, adapter tests, coverage, and release verification.

code.finding: SyncProps controlled selection normalization needed a fix: controlled bindables must be normalized after mode changes.
Ctrl-select toggling from Set::All required a special case to correctly toggle individual items.
Inline autocomplete display text must be computed from the first prefix match in the visible items, not a simple string concatenation.

workflow: Write regression tests before patching behavior so expected failures confirm the test targets the right code path.
When Codex opens new threads mid-CI, fetch and triage them immediately rather than waiting for CI to finish — the new findings can be parallelized with the remaining CI jobs.
Poll CI via gh pr checks --watch=false with sleep intervals calibrated to expected job duration (30-120s) to avoid hammering the API while keeping latency low.
Spec documents must be updated in the same commit as behavior changes to keep the public contract consistent.

friction: Multiple rounds of incremental CI polling were required (10+ poll cycles) because the matrix jobs ran sequentially and took significant cumulative time.
Codex opened 3 new review threads mid-way through CI, requiring an unplanned additional fix cycle after most jobs had already passed.
cargo fmt needed to be run manually after the clippy/test pass because the patch introduced formatting issues, adding an extra step before the final commit.
open_items: Coverage and Release Verification were still pending when Codex opened new threads — the transcript does not confirm their final status after the second push.
The three Codex fixes were committed but the transcript ends before confirming Codex approved or resolved the new threads after the second push.
